seoTitle: How to Always Appear Active on Slack
metaDescription: Keep your Slack presence dot green by manually setting Active status and adjusting system idle timers on desktop and mobile.
intro: Slack's automatic idle timer can flip your status away after just a few minutes of inactivity. This guide covers manually overriding your presence, adjusting system power settings, and other tweaks to keep your status looking active.

steps.heading: Manually set yourself as active
steps.body: Click your profile avatar in the lower left and select 'Set yourself as active' to override the automatic status.

steps.heading: Adjust system power settings
steps.body: Open your computer's Energy Saver or power management panel and extend or disable display sleep timers.

steps.heading: Change mobile auto-lock settings
steps.body: On your phone, open display settings and increase the auto-lock timing to help keep the app from going idle.

steps.heading: Keep Slack visible on screen
steps.body: Run Slack in a split-screen layout alongside your other work windows to maintain regular interaction.

steps.heading: Sync your calendar status for context
steps.body: Connect your calendar so your status reflects meetings accurately instead of relying solely on activity detection.

faq.question: How long before Slack marks me as away automatically?
faq.answer: Slack typically switches to away status after a period of no keyboard or mouse activity, often around 10 to 30 minutes.

faq.question: Does manually setting Active status last permanently?
faq.answer: No, extended inactivity can still eventually override a manual active status, so combining it with system tweaks helps.

faq.question: Is there a downside to always appearing active?
faq.answer: It can set unrealistic expectations for instant responses, so use it thoughtfully rather than to mislead your team.

faq.question: Can admins see if I'm gaming my active status?
faq.answer: Workspace admins generally don't have granular visibility into how your active status was triggered.
